pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Zach's Mustard Boy routine might be leading now, but let's not pretend he's some genius mastermind.** Look, I understand why some might think totaling 16 points and topping the standings means Zach is doing something special with Brazil demolishing Haiti, but frankly, when you're picking teams like Switzerland and the Czech Republic, you're basically playing checkers, not chess. And bless Gayden's heart, the Backpass Assassin is sitting at the bottom with 10 points. He clearly had a plan, I mean, drafting Japan and the Ivory Coast gives off a certain vibe of calculated chaos, but not exactly the winning kind. I feel for Devin too, I really do, especially when you bet heavily on Haiti; you just want to ask, "What were you hoping for, a miracle?" And honestly, Rafe, your choices are an enigma to us all — Germany, and then Uzbekistan? It's like he's throwing darts at a map. I get why Bubba G might be feeling a bit smug tied at 12 points, but if Morocco's lone goal against Scotland is your highlight reel, your strategy could probably use a tweak or two. In this fantasy pool, being in the mix doesn't mean you've cracked the code.

pundit_takes.headline: Germany set to face Ivory Coast in pivotal Group E clash
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden's strategy of drafting non-qualifiers pays off in spirit.

pundit_takes.headline: Netherlands and Sweden to battle in neutral turf showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy still celebrating Brazil's win, turned off notifications.

pundit_takes.headline: Ecuador and Curacao set for low-profile match in Kansas City
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rafe excited to see how his non-existent Ecuador team performs.

pundit_takes.headline: Japan to clash with Tunisia in fierce Group F encounter
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin keen to avenge Turkey's tactical masterclass.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gayden's in free fall with a brutal L2 streak. Backpass Assassin? More like Backpass Crashin'.
Rafe's draft disaster turned miracle; no qualified teams, yet he's in the mix.
Rafe's +6 head-to-head against Devin is the beatdown of the day, no doubt.
Sim favored Gunner, but Rafe the chaos agent crashed the favorite party anyway.
